semantic_description: The function string_fill_char updates a caller-provided char buffer of length n + 1. A for loop writes the character c into each position 0 <= i < n, then the function writes 0 at index n as the terminator. The contract requires n to be nonnegative and within int range, preserves ownership of the full CharArray resource, and ensures the resulting list has length n + 1, every prefix element before n equals c, and the final element equals 0. The empty-loop case n = 0 skips the loop and only writes the terminator.
